findings.title: Audit events have no database-level immutability enforcement
findings.description: The audit_events table remains mutable by any process with database write access. There are no SQLite triggers, row-level policies, or PostgreSQL RLS rules preventing UPDATE or DELETE. An attacker with filesystem or credential access can silently alter or erase the audit trail without detection.

findings.title: Audit events have no hash chain or tamper-evidence fields
findings.description: Each audit row is independent. There is no row_hash, prev_hash, sequence number, or cryptographic commitment. An adversary can insert, delete, or reorder rows without leaving a detectable artifact. The thirteen fields stored per event include no integrity-bearing metadata.

findings.title: Operator token authentication is O(n) with no index on token_hash
findings.description: authenticate_operator() iterates all active operators and runs PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 (600,000 iterations) per entry. No index exists on token_hash. Even a small operator table forces millions of PBKDF2 iterations per request, creating a CPU-exhaustion vector.

findings.title: Structured logging module exists but is not integrated into server.py request path
findings.description: structured_logging.py provides build_log_event(), generate_correlation_id(), and redaction helpers, all implemented and tested in isolation. server.py uses print() for all output. In a production incident there is no structured log stream to query, correlate, or alert on.

findings.title: No migration rollback, dry-run, or concurrent guard
findings.description: Migrations are one-way with no rollback(conn). No preview mode exists. Multiple instances starting simultaneously can race to apply the same migration. A failed migration in production requires manual database recovery.

findings.title: PostgreSQL migrations never validated in CI against a real PostgreSQL instance
findings.description: The persistence design document explicitly states PostgreSQL must be CI-gated. No CI job runs against a real PostgreSQL instance. The SQL placeholder translation layer has never been validated against a live PostgreSQL backend.

findings.title: No rate limiting on authentication or sensitive endpoints
findings.description: No per-IP, per-client, or per-operator rate limiting exists. The O(n) PBKDF2 auth path combined with unlimited request volume creates a trivial CPU-exhaustion DoS vector.

findings.title: Backup/restore and incident response are design-only artifacts
findings.description: Backup/restore and incident response exist as design documents only. No automated backup schedule, no tested restore procedure, no on-call playbook, and no executable runbooks exist. Mean time to recovery is undefined.

findings.title: secret_sources.py is not integrated with config.py secret loading
findings.description: config.py reads secrets directly via os.environ.get(). The safe boundary layer in secret_sources.py (redaction, safe errors, no-value-in-repr) is unused during startup. Secrets are loaded without centralized validation or redaction guarantees.

findings.title: Operator tokens have no expiry, rotation, or last-used tracking
findings.description: Tokens are issued once and never expire. There is no token_expires_at, no last_used_at, and no rotation endpoint. A leaked operator token is valid indefinitely. Bootstrap re-initialization requires manual database row deletion.

findings.title: OpenAPI contract is pre-release and declares only localhost
findings.description: The contract is at 0.31.0-rc (release candidate, not frozen). The servers section contains only http://localhost:8765. No staging or production server URL is declared. No breaking-change policy or deprecation timeline exists.

findings.title: No correlation ID propagation via HTTP headers
findings.description: generate_correlation_id() is implemented but never used. No request handler reads X-Request-ID, generates a correlation ID per request, or includes it in response headers. Cross-system tracing is impossible.

findings.title: No connection pooling for PostgreSQL backend
findings.description: db.py opens a new connection per operation. This is acceptable for SQLite and low-traffic staging but will become a bottleneck under production load on PostgreSQL.

findings.title: No auth failure logging with correlation ID or anomaly detection
findings.description: Repeated authentication failures produce no observable structured signals. There is no application-level backoff, lockout, or alerting on repeated auth failures. A distributed brute-force attack against weak operator tokens would produce no observable signals.

findings.title: server.py monolith will become a maintenance burden
findings.description: server.py contains all HTTP route handlers, middleware, auth guards, request parsing, and startup logic in a single ~1270-line file. This is acceptable for the current phase but will become a maintenance burden as middleware layers are added. Module decomposition should be planned before the next major feature phase.

resolvedRisks.title: CORS wildcard removed
resolvedRisks.evidence: server.py _cors_headers_for() implements exact string matching against config.CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS. Tests confirm no wildcard, no arbitrary origin reflection, andauth requirements remain intact.

resolvedRisks.title: Private key file permission enforcement
resolvedRisks.evidence: crypto_signing.py sets 0o600 at generation and _check_private_key_permissions() rejects mode & 0o077 at load time. Tests verify world-readable, group-readable, and group-writable keys are rejected.

resolvedRisks.title: Self-approval guard encapsulated in grant_requests module
resolvedRisks.evidence: grant_requests.py approve_grant_request() raises ValueError('Self-approval is not permitted') before state mutation. Tests verify state unchanged and no misleading audit event created.

resolvedRisks.title: Denial reason length bounded to 1000 characters
resolvedRisks.evidence: grant_requests.py deny_grant_request() validates len(reason) <= MAX_DENIAL_REASON_LENGTH before state or audit mutation. Overlong reasons are rejected with safe error messages.

resolvedRisks.title: Request expiry trigger implemented with audit trail
resolvedRisks.evidence: expire_old_requests() transitions stale requests to expired and creates audit events with action='expire_grant_request' and approved=False. Tests verify idempotency and correct semantics.

resolvedRisks.title: Approval and revoke audit writes are now transactionally consistent
resolvedRisks.evidence: approve_grant_request() and revoke_grant_request() pass conn=conn to audit_log.append_event(), matching the correct pattern already used by deny_grant_request(). Tests verify rollback on audit failure.

resolvedRisks.title: tamper_grant HTTP endpoint blocked in production
resolvedRisks.evidence: POST /demo/tamper-grant returns 403 when ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS is not set. The direct module call remains available for test simulation. Tests verify blocked by default and accessible only with demo flag + auth.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Audit log immutability at database layer
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Database-level triggers or write-once table are the only mechanism preventing silent audit tampering by direct DB access. This is the highest-priority blocker for a finished product.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Hash chain and tamper-evidence fields on audit events
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Without row_hash and prev_hash, audit tampering is undetectable even with read-only enforcement. Required for forensic compliance claims.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Operator token auth performance and index hardening
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: O(n) PBKDF2 scan is a CPU-exhaustion DoS vector. Must be fixed before operator model can be used with any realistic operator count.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Structured logging integration into server.py request lifecycle
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: structured_logging.py is fully implemented but unused. Production incident diagnosis requires structured output from request handlers and auth paths.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Migration safety: rollback, dry-run, concurrent guard
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: One-way migrations with no preview or concurrency guard are not safe for production deployments or rolling upgrades.

recommendedNextIssues.title: PostgreSQL CI gating for migrations and test suite
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: PostgreSQL is the stated production target but has never been validated in CI. Schema and placeholder translation risks are untested against a real backend.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Rate limiting on auth and mutation endpoints
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Unlimited requests against PBKDF2 auth and mutation endpoints create trivial DoS vectors. Required before accepting untrusted client traffic.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Operational runbooks: backup/restore and incident response
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Design documents are not executable procedures. Production readiness requires tested backup schedules, restore verification, and incident playbooks.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Integrate secret_sources.py with config.py startup path
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: The safe secret boundary layer exists but is bypassed by config loading. Centralized validation and redaction must be active for all secret reads.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Operator token expiry, last-used tracking, and rotation
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Indefinite token validity is a critical security risk. Leaked tokens remain valid forever. Token lifecycle must support expiry and rotation.

recommendedNextIssues.title: OpenAPI contract freeze and versioning policy
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: External integrators cannot rely on a 0.31.0-rc contract. Freeze, version, and document breaking-change policy before any integration commitment.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Correlation ID propagation via HTTP headers
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Cross-system request tracing is impossible without correlation ID propagation. Required for debugging integrations and incident correlation.

recommendedNextIssues.title: PostgreSQL connection pooling
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Single-connection-per-operation design will bottleneck under production PostgreSQL load. Connection pooling is required for throughput.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Auth failure logging and anomaly detection signals
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: Repeated authentication failures produce no structured signals. Detection and alerting on brute-force attempts requires explicit auth failure logging.

recommendedNextIssues.title: Module decomposition plan for server.py monolith
recommendedNextIssues.rationale: The ~1270-line monolith will become unmaintainable as middleware layers grow. Decomposition should be planned before the next major feature phase.
